gallery.ctaBody: Ready to generate your own version instead of browsing examples?

docs.title: How ArkhosAI works, from prompt to production output.
docs.description: The generator is built around a transparent pipeline, typed frontend surfaces, and a backend designed for streaming and reviewability.

quickstart.title: Get started in 3 steps
quickstart.step1Body: Sign up with email or OAuth. The free tier includes 10 generations per month with the Budget fleet profile — no credit card required.
quickstart.step2Body: Write a prompt describing what you want, select a fleet profile (Budget, Balanced, or Quality), and hit Generate. The 5-agent pipeline streams every step in real time.
quickstart.step3Body: Get a production-ready React project with Tailwind CSS, proper file structure, and SEO meta tags. Preview it live in the sandbox, iterate if needed, then download the zip.

start.title: Getting Started
start.body: Account setup, first generation, fleet profile selection, and prompt writing tips. Everything you need to go from zero to a generated website.

pipeline.title: Agent Pipeline
pipeline.body: How the 5-agent system works: Planner analyzes your prompt, Designer and Architect run in parallel, Builder generates React code, and Reviewer checks quality. Each agent streams its output via SSE.

profiles.title: Fleet Profiles
profiles.body: Budget (~€0.004, ~25s), Balanced (~€0.006, ~40s), and Quality (~€0.020, ~90s). Each profile routes different Mistral models to each agent for the optimal cost-quality tradeoff.

sandbox.title: Sandbox Preview
sandbox.body: Live preview runs in an isolated Docker container with Node 22 and pnpm. The sandbox installs dependencies, starts a dev server, and serves your generated site — all within seconds of generation.

api.title: API Reference
api.body: REST endpoints for generation, streaming, results, gallery, and telemetry. SSE events for real-time pipeline progress including agent_start, file_chunk, preview_ready, and generation_complete.

deployment.title: Deployment
deployment.body: Self-host with Docker and docker-compose. Configure Scaleway for production, or run locally with NVIDIA NIM for free GPU inference. Same API, same pipeline, different infrastructure.

apiPreview.title: API at a glance
apiPreview.body: Generate websites with a single POST request. Stream progress via SSE. Download production-ready React projects.

about.title: ArkhosAI is building an EU-native answer to opaque website generation tools.
about.description: The goal is not just to generate pages quickly, but to make the underlying process understandable, sovereign, and extensible.
about.mission:
Most AI website generators treat the generation process as a black box. You type a prompt, wait behind a spinner, and hope for the best. You have no idea what model ran, what it cost, or why it made the decisions it did.

ArkhosAI takes the opposite approach. Every agent in the pipeline streams its work in real time. Every model choice is visible. Every cent of cost is tracked and displayed as it happens. The entire system is open source under MIT, self-hostable, and runs on European infrastructure by default.

We believe the next generation of web development tools should be transparent, composable, and sovereign. Not locked behind proprietary APIs in a jurisdiction you did not choose.

sovereign.title: European by default
sovereign.body: All infrastructure runs on Scaleway in Paris. All AI inference goes through Mistral AI, a French company. Your data never leaves the EU. This is not a feature toggle — it is an architectural decision baked into every layer of the stack.

transparent.title: Transparent generation
transparent.body: The pipeline exposes every step: planning, design, architecture, code generation, and review. You see which model runs each agent, what it costs in EUR, and how long it takes. No spinners hiding opaque API calls.

open.title: Open-core pragmatism
open.body: The entire codebase is MIT-licensed. Self-host it on your own infrastructure, run it on a local GPU with NVIDIA NIM, or use the hosted version. Tramontane, our agent orchestration framework, is a standalone open-source project anyone can use.

team.title: Built in France
team.body: ArkhosAI is built by Jesiel at Bleucommerce SAS in Orléans, France. The project started as a conviction that AI-powered web development should not require sending your data to US infrastructure or paying opaque per-generation fees. Every design decision — from model selection to hosting — reflects this.

opensource.title: Open source at heart
opensource.body: ArkhosAI and Tramontane are both MIT-licensed. The agent pipeline, model routing, skill injection, and telemetry systems are all open. Self-host the full stack with Docker, contribute on GitHub, or build your own agents on top of Tramontane.

legal.contact: For any questions regarding these legal documents, contact us at:

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: How ArkhosAI collects, uses, and protects your personal data. We are committed to transparency and full compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (EU 2016/679).

s1.title: Identity and Contact Details of the Controller
s1.body:
The data controller responsible for processing your personal data is Bleucommerce SAS, a company registered under French law, with its registered office in Orléans, France.

You can reach us at any time regarding data protection matters by writing to privacy@arkhos.ai. For general inquiries, contact hello@arkhos.ai.

As a small and medium-sized enterprise, Bleucommerce SAS is not legally required to appoint a Data Protection Officer under Article 37 of the GDPR. However, we take data protection seriously and handle all requests directly through the email address above.

s2.title: Data We Collect
s2.body:
We collect and process the following categories of personal data when you use ArkhosAI:

Account data: your email address, provided during registration via Supabase authentication. This is required to create and manage your account.

Generation data: the text prompts you submit to generate websites, and the resulting generated code. Prompts are processed by our AI pipeline to deliver the service you requested.

Usage data: technical information such as your IP address, browser type, operating system, pages visited, generation counts, fleet profile selections, and timestamps. This data is collected automatically to ensure service reliability and security.

Preference data: your theme choice (dark, light, or GPU mode), language preference (EN/FR), and cookie consent status. These are stored locally in your browser.

Payment data: if you subscribe to a paid plan, payment processing is handled by Stripe. We do not store your credit card details. We receive only a transaction reference and subscription status from Stripe.

s3.title: Legal Basis for Processing
s3.body:
We process your personal data based on the following legal grounds, pursuant to Article 6(1) of the GDPR:

Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)): processing your account data, prompts, and generated code is necessary to deliver the ArkhosAI service you signed up for. Without this processing, we cannot provide the website generation functionality.

Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)): we process usage data and technical logs to maintain platform security, prevent abuse, enforce rate limits, debug errors, and improve the quality of our service. We have assessed that these interests do not override your fundamental rights, particularly given the minimal and non-sensitive nature of the data involved.

Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)): where we rely on your consent, such as for any future optional analytics or marketing cookies, you may withdraw that consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out before withdrawal.

Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)): we may process certain data to comply with applicable French and EU legal requirements, including tax and accounting obligations.

s4.title: How We Use Your Data
s4.body:
We use your data for the following purposes:

Service delivery: your prompts are sent through our multi-agent AI pipeline (Planner, Designer, Architect, Builder, Reviewer) to generate website code. The generated code is made available to you for preview and download.

Account management: your email address is used to authenticate you, manage your subscription, and communicate essential service updates such as changes to terms or pricing.

Service improvement: aggregated and anonymized usage patterns help us improve the generation pipeline, optimize model routing, and enhance the overall user experience. We do not build individual user profiles for advertising purposes.

Security and abuse prevention: we monitor usage patterns and enforce rate limits (e.g., 10 generations per month on the free tier) to protect the platform from abuse and ensure fair access for all users.

We do not sell your personal data. We do not use your data for third-party advertising. We do not engage in behavioral profiling for marketing purposes.

s5.title: Data Sharing and Third Parties
s5.body:
We share your data only with the following service providers, all of which are established in the European Union:

Mistral AI (Paris, France): your prompts are sent to Mistral AI's API for AI-powered website generation. Mistral AI processes prompts as a data processor on our behalf. Mistral AI does not use your prompts to train their models when accessed via their API.

Supabase (EU region): handles user authentication and account management. Your email address and authentication tokens are processed by Supabase.

Scaleway SAS (Paris, France): hosts our backend infrastructure, sandbox preview containers, and databases. All data is stored in Scaleway's Paris data centers.

Stripe (with EU processing): if you subscribe to a paid plan, Stripe processes your payment information. Stripe is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.

We do not share your data with advertising networks, data brokers, or any other third parties. We do not embed third-party tracking scripts or analytics tools on our platform.

s6.title: International Data Transfers
s6.body:
All your data is processed and stored within the European Union. This is a core architectural decision of ArkhosAI.

Our backend and sandbox infrastructure runs on Scaleway in Paris, France. Our AI provider, Mistral AI, is headquartered in Paris and processes data within the EU. Supabase authentication runs in an EU region.

We do not transfer your personal data to countries outside the European Economic Area (EEA). If this changes in the future, we will ensure appropriate safeguards are in place pursuant to Chapter V of the GDPR (such as Standard Contractual Clauses or an adequacy decision) and will update this policy accordingly.

s7.title: Data Retention
s7.body:
We retain your data only as long as necessary for the purposes described in this policy:

Account data (email, authentication): retained for as long as your account is active. Deleted within 30 days of account deletion.

Generation data (prompts and generated code): retained for 30 days after generation to allow you to access, download, and iterate on your projects. After 30 days, generation data is permanently deleted.

Usage logs (IP addresses, request logs, error logs): retained for 90 days for security monitoring and debugging, then permanently deleted.

Preference data (theme, locale, cookie consent): stored locally in your browser. Cleared when you clear your browser data or revoke consent.

Payment records: retained as required by French tax and accounting law (Articles L. 123-22 et seq. of the Code de commerce), typically 10 years for accounting documents.

s8.title: Your Rights Under GDPR
s8.body:
Under the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

Right of access (Art. 15): you can request a copy of all personal data we hold about you, along with information about how it is processed.

Right to rectification (Art. 16): you can request correction of inaccurate personal data.

Right to erasure (Art. 17): you can request deletion of your personal data, subject to legal retention obligations.

Right to data portability (Art. 20): you can request your data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and have it transmitted to another controller.

Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18): you can request that we limit the processing of your data in certain circumstances.

Right to object (Art. 21): you can object to processing based on legitimate interest at any time. We will cease processing unless we demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds.

Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7(3)): where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw it at any time without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.

These rights apply without discrimination and free of charge.

s9.title: How to Exercise Your Rights
s9.body:
To exercise any of the rights described above, send an email to privacy@arkhos.ai with the subject line "GDPR Request" and a description of your request. Please include enough information for us to verify your identity (typically, the email address associated with your account).

We will acknowledge your request within 7 days and provide a substantive response within 30 days, as required by Article 12(3) of the GDPR. If your request is complex, we may extend this period by an additional 60 days, and we will inform you of the extension and the reasons for it.

If you are unsatisfied with our response, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the French data protection authority: Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL), 3 Place de Fontenoy, TSA 80715, 75334 Paris Cedex 07, France. Website: www.cnil.fr.

s10.title: Automated Decision-Making
s10.body:
ArkhosAI uses artificial intelligence to generate website code based on your text prompts. This involves automated processing through our multi-agent pipeline (powered by Mistral AI models), which plans, designs, and builds websites.

This automated processing does not constitute automated decision-making that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects concerning you within the meaning of Article 22 of the GDPR. The AI generates creative output (website code) based on your instructions, and you retain full control over whether to use, modify, or discard the result.

We do not use automated profiling to make decisions about service access, pricing, or content eligibility. Fleet profile selection (Budget, Balanced, Quality) is chosen by you, not determined automatically based on your personal data.

s11.title: Children's Privacy
s11.body:
ArkhosAI is not directed at children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 16 years of age.

If we become aware that we have collected personal data from a child under 16 without verified parental consent, we will take steps to delete that data as quickly as possible. If you believe a child under 16 has provided us with personal data, please contact us at privacy@arkhos.ai.

s12.title: Changes to This Policy
s12.body:
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or other factors. When we make material changes, we will notify you by posting the updated policy on this page with a revised "Last updated" date.

For significant changes that affect your rights, we will provide additional notice through the email address associated with your account, at least 30 days before the changes take effect.

We encourage you to review this policy periodically. Your continued use of ArkhosAI after the effective date of any changes constitutes your acknowledgment of the updated policy.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: The rules and conditions that apply when you use ArkhosAI. Please read these terms carefully before using the platform.

s1.title: Acceptance of Terms
s1.body:
By accessing or using ArkhosAI (available at arkhos.ai), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, you must not use the service.

These terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and Bleucommerce SAS, the company that operates ArkhosAI. They apply to all users, whether on the free tier or a paid subscription.

If you are using ArkhosAI on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these terms.

s2.title: Description of Service
s2.body:
ArkhosAI is an AI-powered website generator. You provide a text description of the website you want, and our multi-agent pipeline (Planner, Designer, Architect, Builder, Reviewer) generates production-ready React code using Mistral AI models.

The service includes: text-to-website generation via AI agents, real-time streaming of the generation pipeline via Server-Sent Events (SSE), sandbox preview of generated websites in isolated Docker containers, code download as a production-ready React project, and iteration on generated results.

The service is provided as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform. Self-hosting is also available under the MIT license for the open-source components.

s3.title: Account Registration
s3.body:
To use ArkhosAI, you must create an account using a valid email address. Authentication is handled by Supabase.

You agree to provide accurate and complete information during registration and to keep your account credentials secure. You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account.

If you suspect unauthorized access to your account, notify us immediately at hello@arkhos.ai. We are not liable for losses resulting from unauthorized use of your account where you have failed to maintain the security of your credentials.

s4.title: Acceptable Use
s4.body:
You agree to use ArkhosAI only for lawful purposes. Specifically, you must not use the service to:

Generate websites containing illegal content, including but not limited to content that violates intellectual property rights, promotes violence or terrorism, constitutes hate speech or discrimination, or contains child sexual abuse material (CSAM).

Generate malware, phishing pages, scam websites, or any content designed to deceive or harm others.

Attempt to circumvent rate limits, abuse the API, or interfere with the operation of the platform.

Reverse-engineer, decompile, or attempt to extract the proprietary components of the ArkhosAI platform (the open-source components are available under MIT license).

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these rules, with or without prior notice depending on the severity of the violation.

s5.title: Intellectual Property
s5.body:
Generated code: all website code generated by ArkhosAI from your prompts belongs to you. Generated code is provided under the MIT license, meaning you are free to use, modify, distribute, and commercialize it without restriction.

Your prompts: you retain ownership of the text prompts you submit. We process prompts solely to deliver the service and do not claim any intellectual property rights over them.

The ArkhosAI platform: the ArkhosAI platform, including its user interface, design system, backend pipeline, and proprietary algorithms, is owned by Bleucommerce SAS and protected by applicable intellectual property laws. Your use of the service does not grant you any rights to the platform itself.

Tramontane: the Tramontane agent orchestration framework used by ArkhosAI is open-source software released under the MIT license.

shadcn/ui components and other open-source dependencies used in generated code retain their respective licenses.

s6.title: Service Availability and SLA
s6.body:
We strive to maintain high availability of ArkhosAI, but the service is provided on a best-effort basis. We do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation.

We may perform scheduled maintenance that temporarily affects availability. Where possible, we will provide advance notice of planned maintenance.

Generation times and costs are estimates. Actual performance may vary depending on model availability, prompt complexity, server load, and the fleet profile selected. The cost estimates shown during generation are approximations based on token pricing from Mistral AI.

Sandbox previews run in isolated Docker containers with limited lifetimes. Preview URLs are temporary and not intended for production hosting.

s7.title: Limitation of Liability
s7.body:
To the fullest extent permitted by French law, Bleucommerce SAS shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of ArkhosAI.

Our total aggregate liability for any claims arising from your use of the service shall not exceed the total fees you have paid to us in the 12 months preceding the claim.

This limitation applies to all causes of action, whether based on contract, tort, strict liability, or any other legal theory. Nothing in these terms excludes liability for fraud, gross negligence, or any liability that cannot be excluded under applicable law.

Generated code is provided as-is. While our Reviewer agent checks for common issues, we do not guarantee that generated code is free from bugs, security vulnerabilities, or errors. You are responsible for reviewing and testing any generated code before deploying it to production.

s8.title: Termination
s8.body:
You may delete your account at any time by contacting us at hello@arkhos.ai. Upon deletion, we will remove your personal data in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms of Service, engage in abusive behavior, or fail to pay applicable subscription fees. For non-urgent violations, we will provide 14 days' notice and an opportunity to remedy the issue.

Upon termination, your right to access the service ceases immediately. Any generated code you have already downloaded remains yours under the MIT license. Data associated with your account will be deleted according to the retention periods in our Privacy Policy.

s9.title: Governing Law and Disputes
s9.body:
These Terms of Service are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of France, without regard to conflict of law provisions.

Any disputes arising from these terms or your use of ArkhosAI shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Orléans, France.

If you are a consumer within the EU, you also have the right to submit disputes to the Online Dispute Resolution platform provided by the European Commission at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr. Additionally, pursuant to Articles L. 611-1 et seq. of the French Consumer Code, you may refer any unresolved consumer dispute to a certified mediator before bringing it to court.

s10.title: Contact
s10.body:
For questions about these Terms of Service, contact us at:

Bleucommerce SAS
Orléans, France
Email: hello@arkhos.ai

For data protection inquiries specifically, write to privacy@arkhos.ai.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.description: How ArkhosAI uses cookies and local storage. We keep it minimal — only what is necessary to make the product work.

s1.title: What Are Cookies
s1.body:
Cookies are small text files stored on your device by your web browser when you visit a website. They serve various purposes, from remembering your preferences to maintaining your login session.

Local storage is a similar browser technology that allows websites to store data on your device. Unlike cookies, local storage data is not sent to the server with every request.

ArkhosAI uses both cookies and local storage, but only for essential product functionality. We do not use cookies for advertising, behavioral tracking, or cross-site profiling.

s2.title: Cookies We Use
s2.body:
ArkhosAI uses only two categories of cookies: essential cookies that are required for the platform to function, and preference cookies that remember your settings. We currently do not use any analytics or marketing cookies.

This is a deliberate product decision. We believe in privacy by default, and we have chosen not to implement third-party tracking or analytics services. If this changes in the future, we will update this policy and request your consent before setting any non-essential cookies.

s3.title: Essential Cookies
s3.body:
The following cookies and local storage items are essential for ArkhosAI to function. They cannot be disabled without breaking core product functionality:

supabase-auth-token: manages your authentication session. Set by Supabase when you sign in. Expires when you sign out or after the session timeout.

arkhos-theme: stores your selected theme (dark, light, or GPU mode). Stored in local storage. Persists until you change it or clear your browser data.

arkhos-locale: stores your selected language (en or fr). Stored in local storage. Persists until you change it or clear your browser data.

arkhos-cookie-consent: records your cookie consent preferences. Stored in local storage. Persists for 12 months, after which you will be prompted again.

These cookies do not track you across websites, do not contain advertising identifiers, and are not shared with any third party.

s4.title: How to Manage Cookies
s4.body:
You can manage cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies, view what cookies are stored, and set preferences for specific websites.

On ArkhosAI, we display a cookie consent banner on your first visit. You can accept all cookies, accept only essential cookies, or customize your preferences. You can change your preferences at any time through the cookie settings accessible from the footer of every page.

Please note that blocking essential cookies will prevent you from signing in and using the generation features of ArkhosAI.

s5.title: Third-Party Cookies
s5.body:
ArkhosAI does not currently set any third-party cookies. We do not use Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, or any other third-party tracking service.

Supabase authentication may set its own cookies for session management, which are classified as essential and covered in Section 3 above.

If we add analytics or other services in the future that require third-party cookies, we will update this policy, clearly disclose the purpose, and obtain your consent before any non-essential third-party cookies are set, in compliance with the ePrivacy Directive and French CNIL guidelines.

s6.title: Changes to This Policy
s6.body:
We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect changes in our practices or applicable regulations. When we make changes, we will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page.

If we introduce new categories of cookies (such as analytics), we will notify you through the cookie consent banner and request fresh consent as required by Article 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive (2002/58/EC) and CNIL recommendations.

For questions about this Cookie Policy, contact us at privacy@arkhos.ai.

Bleucommerce SAS

In accordance with Article 6 of the Loi n° 2004-575 du 21 juin 2004 pour la confiance dans l'économie numérique (LCEN), this information is made available to all users of the website.

s2.title: Hosting Provider
s2.body:
The ArkhosAI platform is hosted by:

Scaleway SAS
8 rue de la Ville l'Évêque, 75008 Paris, France
Website: https://www.scaleway.com

All data is hosted in Scaleway's Paris (fr-par) data centers within the European Union. Scaleway is a French cloud provider and a subsidiary of the Iliad Group.

s3.title: Intellectual Property
s3.body:
The ArkhosAI platform, including its name, logo, user interface, design system (Electric Indigo), and proprietary backend pipeline, is the intellectual property of Bleucommerce SAS. All rights reserved.

The Tramontane orchestration framework is released under the MIT license and is available as open-source software.

Website code generated by ArkhosAI for users is owned by the respective user and provided under the MIT license. Users are free to use, modify, and distribute their generated code without restriction.

All trademarks, logos, and brand names referenced on this site belong to their respective owners. Mistral AI, Scaleway, Supabase, NVIDIA, and other third-party names are trademarks of their respective companies.

s4.title: Liability
s4.body:
Bleucommerce SAS makes every effort to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the information published on arkhos.ai. However, the information is provided on an as-is basis, and Bleucommerce SAS cannot guarantee that it is complete, accurate, or up to date at all times.

Bleucommerce SAS shall not be held liable for any direct or indirect damages resulting from access to or use of the website, including the use of generated code in production environments.

This website may contain links to external websites. Bleucommerce SAS has no control over the content of these external sites and accepts no responsibility for their content or practices. The presence of a link does not imply endorsement.

s5.title: Applicable Law
s5.body:
This website and its legal notice are governed by French law, in particular:

Loi n° 2004-575 du 21 juin 2004 pour la confiance dans l'économie numérique (LCEN), which establishes obligations for online publishers and hosting providers.

Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation — GDPR), which governs the processing of personal data.

Code de commerce, Articles L. 123-22 et seq., governing commercial record-keeping obligations.

Any disputes relating to this website shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Orléans, France.

sales.title: General Terms of Sale
sales.description: The terms and conditions that apply to paid subscriptions on ArkhosAI (Conditions Générales de Vente). Required under French consumer law.

s1.title: Scope
s1.body:
These General Terms of Sale (Conditions Générales de Vente, or CGV) apply to all paid subscriptions purchased on ArkhosAI (arkhos.ai), operated by Bleucommerce SAS, Orléans, France.

By subscribing to a paid plan, you accept these terms in full. These terms complement the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, which also apply to your use of the platform.

These terms apply to both individual consumers and professional users. Where specific rights apply only to consumers (as defined by Article liminaire of the French Consumer Code), this is stated explicitly.

s2.title: Services and Pricing
s2.body:
ArkhosAI offers the following subscription plans:

Free: no charge, includes 10 generations per month with the Budget fleet profile.

Pro: €12.00 per month (including applicable VAT), includes 100 generations per month with access to all fleet profiles (Budget, Balanced, Quality) and models up to devstral-2 and magistral-small.

Team: €29.00 per month (including applicable VAT), includes 500 generations per month, all fleet profiles, 5 team seats, and priority support.

All prices are displayed in euros (EUR) and include applicable VAT. Bleucommerce SAS reserves the right to modify pricing with 30 days' advance notice. Existing subscribers will be notified by email before any price change takes effect on their next billing cycle.

s3.title: Subscription and Payment
s3.body:
Subscriptions are billed monthly in advance. Payment is processed securely by Stripe, our payment processor.

Accepted payment methods include credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, and other methods supported by Stripe in your region).

Subscriptions automatically renew at the end of each billing period unless cancelled before the renewal date. You will receive a receipt by email for each payment.

If a payment fails, we will attempt to process it again over the following days. If payment continues to fail, your account may be downgraded to the Free tier until a valid payment method is provided.

s4.title: Right of Withdrawal
s4.body:
If you are a consumer within the European Union, you have the right to withdraw from your subscription within 14 days of purchase without giving any reason, pursuant to Articles L. 221-18 et seq. of the French Consumer Code and the EU Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU).

To exercise this right, send a clear statement of your decision to withdraw to hello@arkhos.ai within the 14-day period. You may use the model withdrawal form provided in Annex I of the Consumer Rights Directive, though this is not mandatory.

However, if you have accessed and used the generation service during the withdrawal period, you acknowledge that the performance of the digital content has begun with your prior express consent, and you accept that this may result in the loss of your right of withdrawal for the portion of the service already consumed, in accordance with Article L. 221-28 13° of the Consumer Code.

Refunds for valid withdrawals will be processed within 14 days of receiving your withdrawal request, using the same payment method you used for the original purchase.

s5.title: Service Delivery
s5.body:
Upon successful payment, access to your subscribed plan is activated immediately. You can begin using the additional generation capacity and features right away.

Generations are delivered digitally through the ArkhosAI platform. Each generation produces a set of React source files that you can preview in the sandbox and download as a zip archive.

Generation capacity is measured per calendar month and does not roll over to the following month. Unused generations expire at the end of each billing period.

s6.title: Warranty and Conformity
s6.body:
As a provider of digital content and services, Bleucommerce SAS guarantees that ArkhosAI conforms to the description provided and meets the reasonable expectations of the consumer, in accordance with Articles L. 224-25-12 et seq. of the French Consumer Code.

The legal guarantee of conformity for digital content applies for a period of 2 years from the date of supply, as required by the EU Digital Content Directive (2019/770) as transposed into French law.

If a lack of conformity is identified, you are entitled to request that the service be brought into conformity, or, if that is not possible or disproportionate, to a price reduction or contract termination, in accordance with Articles L. 224-25-17 et seq. of the Consumer Code.

Generated code is provided as a creative AI output. While we strive for quality through our multi-agent pipeline and Reviewer agent, we cannot guarantee that every generation will be free from errors or perfectly match your expectations.

s7.title: Limitation of Liability
s7.body:
To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, Bleucommerce SAS's total liability for claims arising under these General Terms of Sale shall not exceed the total subscription fees paid by you during the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

Bleucommerce SAS shall not be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages, including loss of profits, data loss, or business interruption.

This limitation does not apply to liability arising from intentional misconduct or gross negligence, nor does it affect the statutory rights of consumers under French and EU law, including the legal guarantees described in Section 6.

s8.title: Cancellation and Refunds
s8.body:
You may cancel your subscription at any time. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period. You will retain access to your plan's features until the end of the period you have already paid for.

To cancel, contact us at hello@arkhos.ai or use the account settings page when available.

If you cancel within the 14-day withdrawal period described in Section 4, and you have not yet used any generations, you are entitled to a full refund.

Outside the withdrawal period, no pro-rata refunds are provided for partial months. The subscription simply will not renew at the end of the current period.

Bleucommerce SAS may terminate your subscription in cases of serious or repeated violations of the Terms of Service, with prior notice where reasonably possible.

s9.title: Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
s9.body:
These General Terms of Sale are governed by and construed in accordance with French law.

Any disputes arising from these terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Orléans, France, without prejudice to mandatory consumer protection rules that may grant jurisdiction to the courts of the consumer's place of residence.

Pursuant to Articles L. 611-1 et seq. of the French Consumer Code, if a dispute cannot be resolved through our customer service, you may refer the matter to a certified consumer mediator. The list of certified mediators is available at https://www.economie.gouv.fr/mediation-conso.

You may also use the European Commission's Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) platform at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr.

For disputes relating to personal data, you may lodge a complaint with the Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL) at www.cnil.fr.
